input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK (via MAUI workload or manually)

The `maui` workload installs Android SDK components automatically. To manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was provisioned
dotnet android sdk info

# Or locate it manually (default path)
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
ls $androidSdk

# List installed Android platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Install specific API level if needed (discover available versions first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (required for Android builds)

```powershell
# MAUI bundles Microsoft OpenJDK; verify it's present
dotnet build -t:ResolveJdkPath -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "JdkPath"

# Or install manually via winget (discover versions dynamically)
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Install Windows App SDK runtime (discover latest version)
winget search "Windows App SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6

# Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 6. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# List available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (ensure emulator is running)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Set Up Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install a system image
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_API_35"
```

## 8. (Optional) Install Visual Studio for full IDE support

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop --passive"
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with your SDK version (`dotnet --version` to check).
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command handles most Android SDK/JDK provisioning automatically.
- Run PowerShell as **Administrator** for the registry command and SDK installs.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for Android emulator acceleration: `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`.
